extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates proceedings against the Company, proceedings by the Company, tax proceedings involving the Company, proceedings against Directors, proceedings against Subsidiaries, and proceedings by Subsidiaries. I counted only against rows: criminal proceedings are 1 against the Company plus 4 against Directors; regulatory actions are 2 against the Company plus 1 against Subsidiaries; tax proceedings against the Company are 5 income tax, 3 entry tax, 5 customs/excise/service tax and 6 VAT, totaling 19. For the aggregate amount against the Company and Subsidiaries, I used the quantified against components of ₹47.65 million civil proceedings against the Company, ₹5.26 million regulatory proceedings against the Company, ₹304.88 million tax proceedings involving the Company, and ₹12.00 million regulatory proceedings against Subsidiaries, totaling ₹369.79 million; proceedings by the Company/Subsidiaries and personal Director matters were excluded. The contingent-liabilities figure is the explicitly stated consolidated Total of ₹10,133.99 million as of December 31, 2017, rather than a re-sum. The company-wide top-five customer concentration is 97.83% for Fiscal 2017, based on total income, while the EPC percentages are segment-level and were not used; source monetary tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10 (₹369.79 million = ₹36.979 crore and ₹10,133.99 million = ₹1,013.399 crore).
